title: Product Showcase Configurator with Undo/Redo
description: An interactive product showcase where users configure a survival gear display featuring a damaged helmet and water bottle. Users position, scale, rotate, and assign shelf slots to each product while maintaining a total display-weight budget (conservation law). Full undo/redo history tracks every change.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 (importmap with ES module from cdn.jsdelivr.net). Load DamagedHelmet.glb and WaterBottle.glb via GLTFLoader. Scene setup: a wooden-textured rectangular display shelf (three horizontal slots rendered as BoxGeometry planks) against a neutral gradient background. Use PerspectiveCamera at (0,1.5,4) looking at origin. Add ambient light (0.4), directional light from upper-right (1.0), and a soft hemisphere light. Enable OrbitControls for camera.

Interaction requirements (10-14 steps total):
1. Click-to-select: Raycaster selects either product (highlight with emissive outline).
2. Drag-to-position: Drag selected product along X/Z on its shelf slot using pointer events.
3. Scale slider: An HTML range input (0.5–2.0) scales the selected product. Each product has a 'weight' equal to scale cubed. A top-bar HUD shows total combined weight which must stay ≤ 10.0 (conservation law). If a scale change would exceed budget, clamp it.
4. Rotation knob: A second HTML range input rotates the selected product around Y-axis (0–360°).
5. Shelf-slot assignment: Three buttons (Slot 1/2/3) move the selected product to that slot's position with a smooth tween (use simple lerp in animation loop).
6. Color-tint picker: An HTML color input applies a multiplicative tint to the selected product's materials.
7. Undo/Redo: Two buttons maintain a history stack of state snapshots (position, scale, rotation, tint, slot for both objects). Each interaction pushes a snapshot. Undo restores previous state; redo re-applies. Stack depth at least 20.
8. Multi-input coupling: Changing a product's shelf slot resets its X/Z drag offset to slot center. Scaling one product may auto-reduce the other's max allowed scale to respect the weight budget.
9. Reset button restores both products to default state and clears history.

HUD panel (HTML overlay, top-left): show each product's current weight, total weight, and budget remaining. Style buttons and sliders with a clean dark-themed product-catalog aesthetic. Animate a subtle turntable auto-rotation on the shelf when no product is selected.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  helmetSlotIndex: number,         // which shelf slot (0-2) the helmet occupies
  bottleSlotIndex: number,         // which shelf slot (0-2) the bottle occupies
  helmetScale: number,         // current scale multiplier of the helmet (0.5-2.0)
  bottleScale: number,         // current scale multiplier of the bottle (0.5-2.0)
  helmetRotationY: number,         // helmet Y-axis rotation in radians
  bottleRotationY: number,         // bottle Y-axis rotation in radians
  totalWeight: number,         // combined weight (helmetScale^3 + bottleScale^3)
  selectedProduct: string,         // 'helmet'|'bottle'|'none' indicating current selection
  undoStackSize: number,         // count of actions available to undo
  redoStackSize: number,         // count of actions available to redo
  isDragging: boolean,         // true when user is actively dragging a product
  weightBudgetExceeded: boolean,         // true if totalWeight > 10.0
}
